Subparagraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the taxpayer existed and was a financial institution on each day that is the start day or a subsequent day and on which the parent is a financial institution — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is, in respect of each of its particular taxation years, to determine the value for B in the formulas in subsections (4) and (5) without reference to the start day and days after the start day.

Subsection.Text: If there is an amalgamation (within the meaning assigned by subsection 87(1)) of a taxpayer with one or more other corporations to form one corporation (referred to in this subsection as the “new corporation”), and immediately after the amalgamation the new corporation is a financial institution, in applying subsections (4) and (5) in computing the income of the new corporation for particular taxation years of the new corporation that begin on or after the day on which the amalgamation occurred, the new corporation is, on and after that day, deemed to be the same corporation as and a continuation of the taxpayer in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount included under subsection (2) or deducted under subsection (3) in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year of the taxpayer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount deducted under subsection (4) or included under subsection (5) in computing the taxpayer’s income for a taxation year of the taxpayer that begins before the day on which the amalgamation occurred; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the taxpayer existed and was a financial institution on each day that is the day on which the amalgamation occurred or a subsequent day and on which the new corporation is a financial institution — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the taxpayer’s income.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (9) applies if, at any time, a taxpayer (referred to in this subsection and subsection (9) as the “transferor”) transfers, to a corporation (referred to in this subsection and subsection (9) as the “transferee”) that is related to the transferor, property in respect of a business carried on by the transferor in Canada (referred to in this subsection and subsection (9) as the “transferred business”) and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 138(11.5) or (11.94) applies to the transfer; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 85(1) applies to the transfer, the transfer includes all or substantially all of the property and liabilities of the transferred business and, immediately after the transfer, the transferee is a financial institution.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of the transfer, at any time, of property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferee is, at and after that time, deemed to be the same corporation as and a continuation of the transferor in respect of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount included under subsection (2) or deducted under subsection (3) in computing the transferor’s income for its transition year that can reasonably be attributed to the transferred business,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount deducted under subsection (4) or included under subsection (5) in computing the transferor’s income for a taxation year of the transferor that begins before that time that can reasonably be attributed to the transferred business, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the transferor existed and was a financial institution on each day that includes that time or is a subsequent day and on which the transferee is a financial institution — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the transferor’s income that can reasonably be attributed to the transferred business; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in determining, in respect of the day that includes that time or any subsequent day, any amount that is required under subsection (4) or (5) to be deducted or included in computing the transferor’s income for each particular taxation year from the transferred business, the description of A in the formulas in those subsections is deemed to be nil.

Subsection.Text: If subsection 98(6) deems a partnership (in this subsection referred to as the “new partnership”) to be a continuation of another partnership (in this subsection referred to as the “predecessor partnership”) and, at the time that is immediately after the predecessor partnership ceases to exist, the new partnership is a financial institution, in applying subsections (4) and (5) in computing the income of the new partnership for particular taxation years of the new partnership that begin on or after the day on which it comes into existence, the new partnership is, on and after that day, deemed to be the same partnership as and a continuation of the predecessor partnership in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any amount included under subsection (2) or deducted under subsection (3) in computing the predecessor partnership’s income for its transition year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any amount deducted under subsection (4) or included under subsection (5) in computing the predecessor partnership’s income for a taxation year of the predecessor partnership that begins before the day on which the new partnership comes into existence; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the predecessor partnership existed and was a financial institution on each day that is the day on which the new partnership comes into existence or a subsequent day and on which the new partnership is a financial institution — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the predecessor partnership’s income.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Ceasing to carry on a business
Subsection.Text: If at any time, a taxpayer ceases to be a financial institution

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be deducted, in computing the income of the taxpayer for the taxation year of the taxpayer that includes the time that is immediately before that time, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount included under subsection (2) in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year, and
is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under subsection (4) in computing the income of the taxpayer for a taxation year that began before that time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be included, in computing the income of the taxpayer for the taxation year of the taxpayer that includes the time that is immediately before that time, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount deducted under subsection (3) in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year, and
is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount included under subsection (5) in computing the taxpayer’s income for a taxation year that began before that time.

Subsection.Text: If at any time a taxpayer ceases to exist (otherwise than as a result of a merger to which subsection 87(2) applies, a winding-up to which subsection 88(1) applies or a continuation to which subsection 98(6) applies), for the purposes of subsection (11), the taxpayer is deemed to have ceased to be a financial institution at the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the time (determined without reference to this subsection) at which the taxpayer ceased to be a financial institution, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time that is immediately before the end of the last taxation year of the taxpayer that ended at or before the time at which the taxpayer ceased to exist.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
Section 142.6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Change in status — prescribed payment card corporation share
Subsection.Text: If, at any particular time in a taxation year of a taxpayer that is a financial institution for the taxation year, a property becomes a mark-to-market property of the taxpayer for the taxation year because it ceased, at the particular time, to be a prescribed payment card corporation share of the taxpayer,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to have disposed of the property immediately before the particular time for proceeds of disposition equal to its fair market value immediately before the particular time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to have acquired the property, at the particular time, at a cost equal to those proceeds; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 142.5(1) does not apply to the disposition under subparagraph (a)(i).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Change in status — prescribed securities exchange investment
Subsection.Text: If, at any particular time in a taxation year of a taxpayer that is a financial institution for the taxation year, a property becomes a mark-to-market property of the taxpayer for the taxation year because it ceased, at the particular time, to be a prescribed securities exchange investment of the taxpayer,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to have disposed of the property immediately before the particular time for proceeds of disposition equal to its fair market value immediately before the particular time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to have acquired the property, at the particular time, at a cost equal to those proceeds; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 142.5(1) does not apply to the disposition under subparagraph (a)(i).

Subsection.Text: If, at the end of a particular taxation year of a taxpayer that is a financial institution for the taxation year, the taxpayer holds a share of the capital stock of a corporation, the taxpayer has a significant interest in that corporation at any time in the particular taxation year and the share is mark-to-market property of the taxpayer for the immediately following taxation year, the taxpayer is deemed to have,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: disposed of the share immediately before the end of the particular taxation year for proceeds of disposition equal to the fair market value, at that time, of the share; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: acquired the share at the end of the particular taxation year at a cost equal to those proceeds.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 142.6(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Act, the determination of when a taxpayer acquired a share shall be made without regard to a disposition or acquisition that occurred because of subsection 142.5(2) or subsection (1), (1.1), (1.2), (1.4), (1.5) or (1.6).
Subsection 142.6(1.4) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to taxation years that end after February 22, 1994.
Subsection 142.6(1.5) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to taxation years that begin after 1998.
Subsection 142.6(1.6) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
Subsection (2) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
Subsection 143.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
The definitions in this subsection apply in this section.

Definition.Text: amateur athlete at any time means an individual (other than a trust) who is, at that time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a member of a registered Canadian amateur athletic association;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: eligible to compete, in an international sporting event sanctioned by an international sports federation, as a Canadian national team member; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: not a professional athlete.

Definition.Text: professional athlete means an individual who receives income that is compensation for, or is otherwise attributable to, the individual’s activities as a player or athlete in a professional sport.
qualifying performance income of an individual means income that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is received by the individual in a taxation year in which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual was, at any time, an amateur athlete, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual was not, at any time, a professional athlete;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may reasonably be considered to be in connection with the individual’s participation as an amateur athlete in one or more international sporting events referred to in the definition amateur athlete; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is endorsement income, prize money, or income from public appearances or speeches.

Definition.Text: third party in respect of an arrangement described in paragraph (1.1)(b) means a person who deals at arm’s length with the amateur athlete in respect of the arrangement.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1.2) applies where, at any time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a national sport organization that is a registered Canadian amateur athletic association receives an amount for the benefit of an individual under an arrangement made under rules of an international sport federation that require amounts to be held, controlled and administered by the organization in order to preserve the eligibility of the individual to compete in a sporting event sanctioned by the federation; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an individual enters into an arrangement that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is an account with an issuer described in paragraph (b) of the definition qualifying arrangement in subsection 146.2(1), or that would be so described if that definition applied at that time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: provides that no amount may be deposited, credited or added to the account, other than an amount that is qualifying performance income of the individual or that is interest or other income in respect of the property deposited, credited or added to the account,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: provides that a third party is a mandatory signatory on any payment from the account, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: is not a registered retirement savings plan or a TFSA.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of an arrangement referred to in subsection (1.1),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an inter vivos trust (in this section referred to as the “amateur athlete trust”) is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to be created on the day on which the first amount referred to in paragraph (1.1)(a) or (b) is received by the sport organization or by the issuer, as the case may be, in respect of the arrangement, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to exist until subsection (3) or (4) applies in respect of the trust;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: all property held under the arrangement is deemed to be the property of the amateur athlete trust and not property of any other person;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if, at any time, the sport organization or the issuer, as the case may be, receives an amount under the arrangement and the amount would, in the absence of this subsection, be included in computing the income of the individual in respect of the arrangement for the taxation year that includes that time, the amount is deemed to be income of the amateur athlete trust for that taxation year and not to be income of the individual;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if, at any time, the sport organization or the issuer, as the case may be, pays or transfers an amount under the arrangement to or for the benefit of the individual, the amount is deemed to be an amount distributed at that time to the individual by the amateur athlete trust;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the individual is deemed to be the beneficiary under the amateur athlete trust;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the sport organization or the third party, as the case may be, in respect of the arrangement is deemed to be the trustee of the amateur athlete trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: no tax is payable under this Part by the amateur athlete trust on its taxable income for any taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2008 and subsequent taxation years except that, if the individual in respect of an amateur athlete trust elects under this subsection in writing filed with the Minister of National Revenue on or before the individual’s filing-due date for the 2008 taxation year, in its application to the individual and the amateur athlete trust for the 2008 taxation year, paragraph 143.1(1.2)(c) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if, at any time before March 3, 2009, the sport organization or the issuer, as the case may be, receives an amount under the arrangement and the amount would, in the absence of this subsection, be included in computing the income of the individual in respect of the arrangement for the 2008 taxation year, the amount is deemed to be income of the amateur athlete trust for its 2009 taxation year and not to be income of the individual;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition earned income in subsection 146(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount included under paragraph 56(1)(b), (c.2), (g) or (o) or subparagraph 56(1)(r)(v) in computing the taxpayer’s income for a period in the year throughout which the taxpayer was resident in Canada,

Subsection.Text: The description of D in paragraph (b) of the definition unused RRSP deduction room in subsection 146(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted by the taxpayer,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: under subsection (5) or (5.1) or paragraph 60(v), in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: under paragraph 10 of Article XVIII of the Canada-United States Tax Convention signed at Washington on September 26, 1980 or a similar provision in another tax treaty, in computing the taxpayer’s taxable income for the year, and

Subsection.Text: Section 146 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8.91):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deduction for post-death reduction in value
Subsection.Text: If the annuitant under a registered retirement savings plan dies before the maturity of the plan, there may be deducted in computing the annuitant’s income for the taxation year in which the annuitant dies an amount not exceeding the amount determined, after all amounts payable out of or under the plan have been paid, by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount deemed by subsection (8.8) to have been received by the annuitant as a benefit out of or under the plan,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount (other than an amount described in paragraph (c)) received, after the death of the annuitant, by a taxpayer as a benefit out of or under the plan and included, because of subsection (8), in computing the taxpayer’s income, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: a tax-paid amount in respect of the plan; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts paid out of or under the plan after the death of the annuitant.

Subsection.Text: Except where the Minister has waived in writing the application of this subsection with respect to all or any portion of the amount determined in subsection (8.92) in respect of a registered retirement savings plan, that subsection does not apply if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time after the death of the annuitant, a trust governed by the plan held a non-qualified investment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the last payment out of or under the plan was made after the end of the year following the year in which the annuitant died.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 1997 and subsequent taxation years, except that in its application to the 1997 to 2007 taxation years, paragraph (b) of the definition earned income in subsection 146(1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), is to be read without reference to “or subparagraph 56(1)(r)(v)”.
Subsection (2) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection (3) applies in respect of a registered retirement savings plan in respect of which the last payment out of the plan is made after 2008.
Paragraph (h) of the definition regular eligible amount in subsection 146.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the amount and all other eligible amounts received by the individual in the calendar year that includes the particular time does not exceed $25,000, and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (g) of the definition supplemental eligible amount in subsection 146.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the amount and all other eligible amounts received by the individual in the calendar year that includes the particular time does not exceed $25,000, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years in respect of withdrawals made after January 27, 2009.
Subparagraph (b)(ii) of the definition qualifying arrangement in subsection 146.2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an annuity contract with an issuer that is a licensed annuities provider, or

Subsection.Text: Subsections 146.2(3) to (9) of the Act are replaced by the following:
The conditions in paragraphs (2)(a), (b) and (e) do not apply to the extent that they are inconsistent with subsection (4).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Using TFSA interest as security for a loan
Subsection.Text: A holder of a TFSA may use the holder’s interest or, for civil law, right in the TFSA as security for a loan or other indebtedness if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the terms and conditions of the indebtedness are terms and conditions that persons dealing at arm’s length with each other would have entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be concluded that none of the main purposes for that use is to enable a person (other than the holder) or a partnership to benefit from the exemption from tax under this Part of any amount in respect of the TFSA.

Subsection.Text: If the issuer of an arrangement that is, at the time it is entered into, a qualifying arrangement files with the Minister, before March of the calendar year following the calendar year in which the arrangement was entered into, an election in prescribed form and manner to register the arrangement as a TFSA under the Social Insurance Number of the individual with whom the arrangement was entered into, the arrangement becomes a TFSA at the time the arrangement was entered into and ceases to be a TFSA at the earliest of the following times:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the time at which the last holder of the arrangement dies;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time at which the arrangement ceases to be a qualifying arrangement; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the earliest time at which the arrangement is not administered in accordance with the conditions in subsection (2).

Subsection.Text: No tax is payable under this Part by a trust that is governed by a TFSA on its taxable income for a taxation year, except that, if at any time in the taxation year, it carries on one or more businesses or holds one or more properties that are non-qualified investments (as defined in subsection 207.01(1)) for the trust, tax is payable under this Part by the trust on the amount that would be its taxable income for the taxation year if it had no incomes or losses from sources other than those businesses and properties, and no capital gains or capital losses other than from dispositions of those properties, and for that purpose,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: “income” includes dividends described in section 83; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trust’s taxable capital gain or allowable capital loss from the disposition of a property is equal to its capital gain or capital loss, as the case may be, from the disposition.

Subsection.Text: An amount that is credited or added to a deposit that is a TFSA as interest or other income in respect of the TFSA is deemed not to be received by the holder of the TFSA solely because of that crediting or adding.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Trust ceasing to be a TFSA
Subsection.Text: If an arrangement that governs a trust ceases, at a particular time, to be a TFSA,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the trust is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to have disposed, immediately before the particular time, of each property held by the trust for proceeds equal to the property’s fair market value immediately before the particular time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to have acquired, at the particular time, each such property at a cost equal to that fair market value;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trust’s last taxation year that began before the particular time is deemed to have ended immediately before the particular time; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a taxation year of the trust is deemed to begin at the particular time.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Trust ceasing to be a TFSA on death of holder
Subsection.Text: If an arrangement that governs a trust ceases to be a TFSA because of the death of the holder of the TFSA,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement is deemed, for the purposes of subsections (6) and (8), any regulations made under subsection (13), the definition trust in subsection 108(1), paragraph 149(1)(u.2) and the definitions qualified investment and non-qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1), to continue to be a TFSA until, and to cease to be a TFSA immediately after, the exemption-end time, being in this subsection the earlier of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the time at which the trust ceases to exist, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the end of the first calendar year that begins after the holder dies;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be included in computing a taxpayer’s income for a taxation year the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of a payment made out of or under the trust, in satisfaction of all or part of the taxpayer’s beneficial interest in the trust, in the taxation year, after the holder’s death and at or before the exemption-end time, and
is an amount designated by the trust not exceeding the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount of the payment, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount by which the fair market value of all of the property held by the trust immediately before the holder’s death exceeds the total of all amounts each of which is the value of B in respect of any other payment made out of or under the trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be included in computing the trust’s income for its first taxation year, if any, that begins after the exemption-end time the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of all of the property held by the trust at the exemption-end time, and

is the amount by which the fair market value of all of the property held by the trust immediately before the holder’s death exceeds the total of all amounts each of which is the value of B in paragraph (b) in respect of a payment made out of or under the trust.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Annuity contract ceasing to be a TFSA
Subsection.Text: If an annuity contract ceases, at a particular time, to be a TFSA,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the holder of the TFSA is deemed to have disposed of the contract immediately before the particular time for proceeds equal to its fair market value immediately before the particular time;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the contract is deemed to be a separate annuity contract issued and effected at the particular time otherwise than pursuant to or as a TFSA; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each person who has an interest or, for civil law, a right in the separate annuity contract at the particular time is deemed to acquire the interest at the particular time at a cost equal to its fair market value at the particular time.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deposit ceasing to be a TFSA
Subsection.Text: If a deposit ceases, at a particular time, to be a TFSA,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the holder of the TFSA is deemed to have disposed of the deposit immediately before the particular time for proceeds equal to its fair market value immediately before the particular time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each person who has an interest or, for civil law, a right in the deposit at the particular time is deemed to acquire the interest at the particular time at a cost equal to its fair market value at the particular time.

Subsection.Text: An arrangement that is a qualifying arrangement at the time it is entered into is deemed not to be a retirement savings plan, an education savings plan, a retirement income fund or a disability savings plan.
The Governor in Council may make regulations requiring issuers of TFSAs to file information returns in respect of TFSAs.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Section 146.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
The minimum amount under a retirement income fund for 2008 is 75 per cent of the amount that would, in the absence of this subsection, be the minimum amount under the fund for the year.
Subsection (1.1) does not apply to a retirement income fund

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections (5.1) and 153(1) and the definition periodic pension payment in section 5 of the Income Tax Conventions Interpretation Act; nor

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the individual who was the annuitant under the fund on January 1, 2008 attained 70 years of age in 2007.

Subsection.Text: Section 146.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6.2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Deduction for post-death reduction in value
Subsection.Text: If the last annuitant under a registered retirement income fund dies, there may be deducted in computing the annuitant’s income for the taxation year in which the annuitant dies an amount not exceeding the amount determined, after all amounts payable out of or under the fund have been paid, by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount deemed by subsection (6) to have been received by the annuitant out of or under the fund,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount (other than an amount described in paragraph (c)) received, after the death of the annuitant, by a taxpayer out of or under the fund and included, because of subsection (5), in computing the taxpayer’s income, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount that would, if the fund were a registered retirement savings plan, be a tax-paid amount (within the meaning assigned by subsection 146(1)) in respect of the fund; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts paid out of or under the fund after the death of the annuitant.

Subsection.Text: Except where the Minister has waived in writing the application of this subsection with respect to all or any portion of the amount determined in subsection (6.3) in respect of a registered retirement income fund, that subsection does not apply if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time after the death of the annuitant, a trust governed by the fund held an investment that is not a qualified investment; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the last payment out of or under the fund was made after the end of the year following the year in which the annuitant died.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies in respect of a registered retirement income fund in respect of which the last payment out of the fund is made after 2008.
The definition entrusted shares percentage in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is repealed.
The definition divestment obligation percentage in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: divestment obligation percentage of a private foundation for a particular taxation year, in respect of a class of shares of the capital stock of a corporation, is the percentage, if any, that is the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the excess, if any, at the end of the taxation year, of the percentage of issued and outstanding shares of that class that are held by the private foundation over the exempt shares percentage of the private foundation, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the percentage determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the percentage determined under this paragraph in respect of the private foundation in respect of the class for the preceding taxation year,

is the total of all percentages, each of which is the portion of a net increase in the excess corporate holdings percentage of the private foundation in respect of the class for the particular taxation year or for a preceding taxation year that is allocated to the particular taxation year in accordance with subsection 149.2(5), and

is the total of all percentages, each of which is the portion of a net decrease in the excess corporate holdings percentage of the private foundation in respect of the class for the particular taxation year or for a preceding taxation year that is allocated to the particular taxation year in accordance with subsection 149.2(7);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition excess corporate holdings percentage in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the number of percentage points, if any, by which the total corporate holdings percentage of the private foundation in respect of the class, at that time, exceeds the greater of 20% and the exempt shares percentage, at that time, of the private foundation in respect of the class;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: equity percentage of a person in a corporation has, subject to subsection 149.2(2.1), the same meaning as defined in subsection 95(4);
exempt shares held by a private foundation at any particular time means shares, of a class of the capital stock of a corporation,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that were acquired by the private foundation by way of a gift that was subject to a trust or direction that the shares are to be held by the private foundation for a period ending not earlier than the particular time, if the gift was made

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: before March 19, 2007,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: on or after March 19, 2007 and before March 19, 2012

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: under the terms of a will that was executed by a taxpayer before March 19, 2007 and not amended, by codicil or otherwise, on or after March 19, 2007, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in circumstances where no other will of the taxpayer was executed or amended on or after March 19, 2007, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: on or after March 19, 2007, under the terms of a testamentary or inter vivos trust created before March 19, 2007, and not amended on or after March 19, 2007,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that were last acquired by the private foundation before March 19, 2007, other than shares that, at the particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: are described in paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: are listed on a designated stock exchange, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: are shares of the capital stock of a particular corporation, which particular corporation has an equity percentage greater than 0% in a public corporation, a class of the shares of the capital stock of which is listed on a designated stock exchange, if

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a corporation (in this subparagraph referred to as a “controlled corporation” and which may, for greater certainty, be the particular corporation)

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: owns one or more shares of a class of the capital stock of the public corporation, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: is controlled, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, by one or more relevant persons in respect of the private foundation, or by the private foundation alone or together with one or more such relevant persons,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the private foundation, if it held directly the shares described in subclause (A)(I), would have an excess corporate holdings percentage (determined without reference to subsection 149.2(8)) in respect of that class of shares that is greater than 0%, and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the private foundation, alone or together with all controlled corporations, holds more than an insignificant interest in respect of the class of shares described in subclause (A)(I), or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that are substituted shares held by the private foundation;

Definition.Text: exempt shares percentage of a private foundation at any time, in respect of a class of shares of the capital stock of a corporation, is the total of all amounts, each of which is the percentage of the issued and outstanding shares of that class that are exempt shares held by the private foundation at that time;

substituted shares held by a private foundation means shares acquired by the private foundation, in exchange for exempt shares held by the private foundation, in the course of a transaction to which section 51, subsection 85.1(1) or section 86 or 87 applies;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(15) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (b), by adding “and’’ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Minister, or a Minister referred to in paragraph 110.1(8)(e), may make available to the public in any manner a listing of the registered charities in respect of which an opinion has been formed for the purpose of paragraph 110.1(8)(e) or revoked under subsection 110.1(9).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) apply after March 18, 2007.
Section 149.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

For the purposes of the definition equity percentage, and subparagraph (b)(iii) of the definition exempt shares, in subsection 149.1(1), a person who, if paragraph 251(5)(b) applied would be deemed by that paragraph to have the same position in relation to the control of a corporation as if the person owned a share, is deemed to own the share.
Section 149.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Subsection (10) applies for the purposes of applying section 149.1 and subsections (8) and 188.1(3.1) to a private foundation at a particular time if, both on March 18, 2007 and at the particular time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the private foundation was the sole trustee of a trust, or was a majority interest beneficiary (within the meaning assigned by section 251.1) of a trust more than 50% of the trustees of which were the private foundation and one or more relevant persons in respect of the private foundation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trust held one or more shares of a class of the capital stock of a corporation.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Shares held through a trust on March 18, 2007
Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies at a particular time to a private foundation in respect of shares of a class of the capital stock of a corporation held by a trust, the private foundation is deemed to hold at the particular time that number of those shares as is determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of the number of those shares held by the trust on March 18, 2007 and the number so held at the particular time;
is the total fair market value of all interests held by the private foundation in the trust at the particular time; and
is the total fair market value of all property held by the trust at the particular time.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsection (10), if the amount of income or capital of a trust that a person may receive as a beneficiary under the trust depends on the exercise by any person of, or the failure by any person to exercise, a discretionary power, that person is deemed to have fully exercised, or to have failed to exercise, the power, as the case may be.
Subsection (1) applies after March 18, 2007.
Subsection (2) applies to taxation years, of private foundations, that begin on or after February 26, 2008.

If a registered charity was on March 19, 2007 a private foundation, in applying paragraphs 149.2(5)(b) and (c) of the Act to the first taxation year of the registered charity that begins after that date, the reference in those paragraphs to “in the current year” shall be read as a reference to “in the period that begins on March 18, 2007 and ends at the end of the current year”.
Section 150.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Mandatory filing of return by electronic transmission
Subsection.Text: If a corporation is, in respect of a taxation year, a prescribed corporation, the corporation shall file its return of income for the taxation year by way of electronic filing.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after 2009.
Subsection 152(4) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a) and by adding the following after paragraph (b):

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer or person filing the return has filed with the Minister a waiver in prescribed form within the additional 3-year period referred to in paragraph (b); or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: as a consequence of a change in the allocation of the taxpayer’s taxable income earned in a province as determined under the law of a province that provides rules similar to those prescribed for the purposes of section 124, an assessment, reassessment or additional assessment of tax for a taxation year payable by a corporation under a law of a province that imposes on the corporation a tax similar to the tax imposed under this Part (in this paragraph referred to as a “provincial reassessment”) is made, and as a consequence of the provincial reassessment, an assessment, reassessment or additional assessment is made on or before the day that is one year after the later of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which the Minister is advised of the provincial reassessment, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the day that is 90 days after the day of mailing of a notice of the provincial reassessment.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 152(4.01) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Assessment to which paragraph 152(4)(a), (b) or (c) applies
Subsection.Text: Notwithstanding subsections (4) and (5), an assessment, reassessment or additional assessment to which paragraph (4)(a), (b) or (c) applies in respect of a taxpayer for a taxation year may be made after the taxpayer’s normal reassessment period in respect of the year to the extent that, but only to the extent that, it can reasonably be regarded as relating to,
The portion of paragraph 152(4.01)(b) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where paragraph 4(b) or (c) applies to the assessment, reassessment or additional assessment,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 152(6) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (f.2):

Paragraph.Label: (f.3)
Paragraph.Text: a deduction (including for the purposes of this subsection a reduction of an amount otherwise required to be included in computing a taxpayer’s income) under subsection 146(8.9) or (8.92) or 146.3(6.2) or (6.3),

Subsection.Text: Section 152 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6.1):

The Minister shall reassess a taxpayer’s tax for a particular taxation year, in order to take into account the application of paragraph (d) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1), or the application of subsection 142.6(1.6), in respect of property held by the taxpayer, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer has filed for the particular taxation year the return of income required by section 150; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer files with the Minister a prescribed form amending the return, on or before the filing-due date for the taxpayer’s taxation year that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the filing is in respect of paragraph (d) of that definition excluded property, includes the acquisition of control time referred to in that paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the filing is in respect of subsection 142.6(1.6), immediately follows the particular taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (4) applies in respect of a registered retirement income fund or a registered retirement savings plan in respect of which the last payment out of the fund or plan is made after 2008.
Subsection (5) applies to taxation years that begin after 2001, except that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for taxation years that begin before October 1, 2006, each reference in subsection 152(6.2) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (5), to paragraph (d) of the definition excluded property shall be read as a reference to paragraph (d.3) of the definition mark-to-market property; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed form referred to in paragraph 152(6.2)(b) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (5), is deemed to have been filed by a taxpayer on a timely basis if it is filed by the taxpayer on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the taxpayer’s taxation year that includes the day on which this Act is assented to.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 157(1.2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for which the amount determined under subsection (1.3) for the taxation year, or for the preceding taxation year, does not exceed $500,000;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years ending after 2008, except that for taxation years that end in 2009, paragraph 157(1.2)(a) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (1), shall be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined under subsection (1.3) for the taxation year does not exceed the amount that is the total of $400,000 and that proportion of $100,000 that the number of days in the taxation year that are in 2009 is of the number of days in the taxation year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined under subsection (1.3) for the preceding taxation year does not exceed $400,000;

Subsection.Text: Section 162 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Late filing penalty — prescribed information returns
Subsection.Text: Every person (other than a registered charity) or partnership who fails to file, when required by this Act or the regulations, one or more information returns of a type prescribed for the purpose of this subsection is liable to a penalty equal to the greater of $100 and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is less than 51, $10 multiplied by the number of days, not exceeding 100, during which the failure continues;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 50 and less than 501, $15 multiplied by the number of days, not exceeding 100, during which the failure continues;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 500 and less than 2,501, $25 multiplied by the number of days, not exceeding 100, during which the failure continues;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 2,500 and less than 10,001, $50 multiplied by the number of days, not exceeding 100, during which the failure continues; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 10,000, $75 multiplied by the number of days, not exceeding 100, during which the failure continues.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to file in appropriate manner — prescribed information returns
Subsection.Text: Every person (other than a registered charity) or partnership who fails to file, in the manner required by the regulations, one or more information returns of a type prescribed for the purpose of this subsection is liable to a penalty equal to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 50 and less than 251, $250;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 250 and less than 501, $500;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 500 and less than 2,501, $1,500;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: where the number of those information returns is greater than 2,500, $2,500; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, nil.

Subsection.Text: Section 162 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (7.1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Failure to file in appropriate manner — return of income
Subsection.Text: Every person who fails to file a return of income for a taxation year as required by subsection 150.1(2.1) is liable to a penalty equal to $1,000.
Subsection (1) applies to returns required to be filed after 2009.

Subsection (2) applies to taxation years that end after 2010 except that, in its application to the 2011 and 2012 taxation years, the reference to $1,000 in subsection 162(7.2) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is to be read as

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: $250, for the 2011 taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: $500, for the 2012 taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Clause 181.3(1)(c)(ii)(B) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
Paragraph 188.1(3.2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each of those shares is deemed to have a fair market value, at the particular time, equal to the fair market value, at the particular time, of a share of the class issued by the corporation, determined without reference to this subsection.

Subsection.Text: Section 188.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3.2):
Subsection (3.5) applies to a private foundation at a particular time in a taxation year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the particular time, a person (in this subsection and subsection (3.5) referred to as an “insider” of the private foundation) that is the private foundation, or is a relevant person in respect of the private foundation, is a beneficiary under a trust;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: at or before the particular time

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the insider acquired an interest in or under the trust, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust acquired a property;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it may reasonably be considered that a purpose of the acquisition described in paragraph (b) was to hold, directly or indirectly, shares of a class of the capital stock of a corporation (referred to in subsection (3.5) as the “subject corporation”);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the shares described in paragraph (c) would, if they were held by the insider, cause the private foundation to have a divestment obligation percentage for the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: at the particular time, the insider holds the interest described in subparagraph (b)(i), or the trust holds the property described in subparagraph (b)(ii), as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of subsections (3.3) and (3.5),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: interests (or, for civil law, rights), other than shares, of a trust in a corporation that entitle the trust to a right described in paragraph 251(5)(b) in respect of a class of the capital stock of the corporation, are deemed to be converted into shares of that class in the manner described by paragraph (3.2)(a); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the amount of income or capital of the trust that a person may receive as a beneficiary under the trust depends on the exercise by any person of, or the failure by any person to exercise, a discretionary power, that person is deemed to have fully exercised, or to have failed to exercise, the power, as the case may be.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to a private foundation at a particular time in respect of an interest of an insider of the private foundation in a trust, for the purposes of applying this section, subsection 149.1(1) and section 149.2,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the insider is deemed to hold at the particular time, in addition to any shares of the capital stock of the subject corporation that it holds otherwise than because of this subsection, the number of shares, of the class of shares referred to in paragraph (3.3)(c), determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of shares of that class that are held, directly or indirectly, by the trust at the particular time,
is the total fair market value of all interests held by the insider in the trust at the particular time, and
is the total fair market value of all property held by the trust at the particular time;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: each of those shares is deemed to be a share that is issued by the subject corporation and outstanding and to continue to be held by the holder until such time as the holder no longer holds the interest or right; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each of those shares is deemed to have a fair market value, at the particular time, equal to the fair market value, at the particular time, of a share of the class issued by the subject corporation, determined without reference to this subsection.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years, of private foundations, that begin on or after February 26, 2008.
Subparagraph 190.11(b)(ii) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
Subparagraph 190.13(c)(iv) of the Act is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
The portion of the definition SIFT partnership in subsection 197(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: société de personnes intermédiaire de placement déterminée

Definition.Text: SIFT partnership, being a specified investment flow-through partnership, for any taxation year, means a partnership other than an excluded subsidiary entity (as defined in subsection 122.1(1)) for the taxation year that meets the following conditions at any time during the taxation year:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on October 31, 2006.
Paragraph (c.1) of the definition qualified investment in section 204 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: debt obligations that meet the following criteria, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the debt obligations had, at the time of acquisition by the trust, an investment grade rating with a prescribed credit rating agency,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the debt obligations have an investment grade rating with a prescribed credit rating agency, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: the debt obligations were acquired by the trust in exchange for debt obligations that satisfied the condition in clause (A) and as part of a proposal to, or an arrangement with, the creditors of the issuer of the debt obligations that has been approved by a court under the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act or the Companies’ Creditors Arrangement Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: either

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the debt obligations were issued as part of a single issue of debt of at least $25 million, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in the case of debt obligations that are issued on a continuous basis under a debt issuance program, the issuer of the debt obligations had issued and outstanding debt under the program of at least $25 million,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in determining whether a property is, at any time after March 18, 2007, a qualified investment.
The portion of clause 204.81(1)(c)(ii)(A) of the Act before subclause (I) is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: Class A shares that are issuable only to individuals (other than trusts), trusts governed by registered retirement savings plans and trusts governed by TFSAs and that entitle their holders

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The definition restricted property in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is repealed.

Paragraph (a) of the definition advantage in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraphs (i) and (ii) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ii):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a distribution under the TFSA, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the payment or allocation of any amount to the TFSA by the issuer; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition advantage in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a benefit that is an increase in the total fair market value of the property held in connection with the TFSA if it is reasonable to consider, having regard to all the circumstances, that the increase is attributable, directly or indirectly, to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a transaction or event or a series of transactions or events that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: would not have occurred in an open market in which parties deal with each other at arm’s length and act prudently, knowledgeably and willingly, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: had as one of its main purposes to enable a person or a partnership to benefit from the exemption from tax under Part I of any amount in respect of the TFSA, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a payment received as, on account or in lieu of, or in satisfaction of, a payment

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: for services provided by a person who is, or who does not deal at arm’s length with, the holder of the TFSA, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: of interest, of a dividend, of rent, of a royalty or of any other return on investment, or of proceeds of disposition, in respect of property (other than property held in connection with the TFSA) held by a person who is, or who does not deal at arm’s length with, the holder of the TFSA; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a prescribed benefit.

Subsection.Text: The description of E in the definition excess TFSA amount in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the qualifying portion of a distribution made in the calendar year and at or before the particular time under a TFSA of which the individual was the holder at the time of the distribution and, for this purpose, the qualifying portion of a distribution is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: nil, if the distribution is a qualifying transfer or a prescribed distribution, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount of the distribution, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount that would be the individual’s excess TFSA amount at the time of the distribution if the amount of the distribution were nil.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition prohibited investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: prohibited investment, at any time, for a trust governed by a TFSA means property (other than prescribed property) that is at that time

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition prohibited investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: prescribed property.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: exempt contribution means a contribution made in a calendar year under a TFSA by the survivor of an individual if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the contribution is made during the period (in this definition referred to as the “rollover period”) that begins when the individual dies and that ends at the end of the first calendar year that begins after the individual dies (or at any later time that is acceptable to the Minister);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a payment (in this definition referred to as the “survivor payment”) was made to the survivor during the rollover period, as a consequence of the individual’s death, directly or indirectly out of or under an arrangement that ceased, because of the individual’s death, to be a TFSA;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the survivor designates, in prescribed form filed in prescribed manner within 30 days after the day on which the contribution is made, the contribution in relation to the survivor payment; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of the contribution does not exceed the least of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the amount of the survivor payment

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the total of all other contributions designated by the survivor in relation to the survivor payment,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount, if any, by which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the total proceeds of disposition that would, if section 146.2 were read without reference to subsection 146.2(9), be determined in respect of the arrangement under paragraph 146.2(8)(a), (10)(a) or (11)(a), as the case may be,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: the total of all other exempt contributions in respect of the arrangement made by the survivor at or before the time of the contribution, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the individual had, immediately before the individual’s death, an excess TFSA amount or if payments described in paragraph (b) are made to more than one survivor of the individual, nil or the greater amount, if any, allowed by the Minister in respect of the contribution.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 207.01(2) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 207.01(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If an individual’s survivor becomes the holder of a TFSA as a consequence of the individual’s death and, immediately before the individual’s death, the individual had an excess TFSA amount, the survivor is deemed (other than for the purposes of the definition exempt contribution) to have made, at the beginning of the month following the individual’s death, a contribution under a TFSA equal to the amount, if any, by which
Subsections (1) to (9) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The portion of section 207.03 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If, at a particular time, a non-resident individual makes a contribution under a TFSA (other than a contribution that is a qualifying transfer or an exempt contribution), the individual shall pay a tax under this Part equal to 1% of the amount of the contribution in respect of each month that ends after the particular time and before the earlier of

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 207.04(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Where both prohibited and non-qualified investment
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section and subsection 146.2(6), if a trust governed by a TFSA holds property at any time that is, for the trust, both a prohibited investment and a non-qualified investment, the property is deemed at that time not to be a non-qualified investment, but remains a prohibited investment, for the trust.
The portion of subsection 207.04(7) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:
The amount of tax payable under subsection (6) for a calendar year is 150% of the amount of tax that would be payable under Part I by the trust for the taxation year that ends in the calendar year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Act were read without reference to paragraph 82(1)(b), section 121 and subsection 146.2(6); and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The portion of subsection 207.06(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a person would otherwise be liable to pay a tax under this Part because of subsection 207.04(1) or section 207.05, the Minister may waive or cancel all or part of the liability where the Minister considers it just and equitable to do so having regard to all the circumstances, including
Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph (a) of the definition registered life insurance policy in subsection 211(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as a registered retirement savings plan, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph 212(1)(p) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that would, if the non-resident person had been resident in Canada at the time at which the amount was paid, be required by paragraph 12(1)(z.5) to be included in computing the non-resident person’s income for the taxation year that includes that time;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The definition assessable distribution in subsection 218.3(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: assessable distribution, in respect of a Canadian property mutual fund investment, means the portion of any amount that is paid or credited (otherwise than as a SIFT trust wind-up event), by the mutual fund that issued the investment, to a non-resident investor who holds the investment, and that is not otherwise subject to tax under Part I or Part XIII.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies after July 14, 2008.
The portion of subsection 241(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Except as authorized by this section, no official or other representative of a government entity shall
Subsection 241(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Notwithstanding any other Act of Parliament or other law, no official or other representative of a government entity shall be required, in connection with any legal proceedings, to give or produce evidence relating to any taxpayer information.
Paragraph 241(4)(l) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (9.2), provide to a representative of a government entity the business number of, the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), and any contact information, corporate information and registration information in respect of, the holder of a business number (other than an excluded individual), if the information is provided solely for the purposes of the administration or enforcement of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an Act of Parliament or of a legislature of a province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a by-law of a municipality in Canada or a law of an aboriginal government;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 241(5) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure to taxpayer or on consent
Subsection.Text: An official or other representative of a government entity may provide taxpayer information relating to a taxpayer
The portion of subsection 241(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An order or direction that is made in the course of or in connection with any legal proceedings and that requires an official, other representative of a government entity or authorized person to give or produce evidence relating to any taxpayer information may, by notice served on all interested parties, be appealed forthwith by the Minister or by the person against whom the order or direction is made to
Section 241 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (9.1):

No information may be provided to a representative of a government entity under paragraph (4)(l) in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the government entity unless the government entity uses the business number as an identifier in connection with the program, activity or service.

The Minister may, in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the Minister, make available to the public the business number of, and the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), the holder of a business number (other than an excluded individual).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Public disclosure by representative of government entity
Subsection.Text: A representative of a government entity may, in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the government entity, make available to the public the business number of, and the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), the holder of a business number (other than an excluded individual), if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a representative of the government entity was provided with that information pursuant to paragraph (4)(l); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the government entity uses the business number as an identifier in connection with the program, activity or service.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition taxpayer information in subsection 241(10) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: but does not include information that does not directly or indirectly reveal the identity of the taxpayer to whom it relates and, for the purposes of applying subsections (2), (5) and (6) to a representative of a government entity that is not an official, taxpayer information includes only the information referred to in paragraph (4)(l);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 241(10) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: aboriginal government means an aboriginal government as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act;

contact information, in respect of a holder of a business number, means the name, address, telephone number, facsimile number and preferred language of communication of the holder, or similar information as specified by the Minister in respect of the holder, and includes such information in respect of one or more

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: trustees of the holder, if the holder is a trust,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: members of the holder, if the holder is a partnership,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: officers of the holder, if the holder is a corporation, or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: officers or members of the holder, if the holder is not described by any of paragraphs (a) to (c);

Definition.Text: corporate information, in respect of a holder of a business number that is a corporation, means the name (including the number assigned by the incorporating authority), date of incorporation, jurisdiction of incorporation and any information on the dissolution, reorganization, amalgamation, winding-up or revival of the corporation;

excluded individual means an individual who is a holder of a business number solely because the individual is required under this Act to deduct or withhold an amount from an amount paid or credited or deemed to be paid or credited;
government entity means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a department or agency of the government of Canada or of a province,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a municipality in Canada,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an aboriginal government,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation all of the shares (except directors’ qualifying shares) of the capital stock of which are owned by one or more persons each of which is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of a province,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a municipality in Canada, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation described in this paragraph, or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a board or commission, established by Her Majesty in right of Canada or Her Majesty in right of a province, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of government, or by one or more municipalities in Canada, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of a municipality;

Definition.Text: registration information, in respect of a holder of a business number, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any information pertaining to the legal form of the holder,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the type of activities carried on or proposed to be carried on by the holder,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each date on which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the business number was issued to the holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder began activities,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder ceased or resumed activities, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the business number assigned to the holder was changed, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons for the cessation, resumption or change referred to in subparagraph (c)(iii) or (iv);

Definition.Text: representative of a government entity means a person who is employed in the service of, who occupies a position of responsibility in the service of, or who is engaged by or on behalf of, a government entity, and includes, for the purposes of subsections (1), (2), (5) and (6), a person who was formerly so employed, who formerly occupied such a position or who formerly was so engaged;

Subsection.Text: The definition functional currency in subsection 248(1) of the Act is repealed.
The definitions amateur athlete trust, personal trust and TFSA in subsection 248(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: fiducie au profit d’un athlète amateur

Definition.Text: amateur athlete trust has the meaning assigned by subsection 143.1(1.2);
personal trust means a trust (other than a trust that is, or was at any time after 1999, a unit trust) that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a testamentary trust, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an inter vivos trust no beneficial interest in which was acquired for consideration payable directly or indirectly to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the trust, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: any person or partnership that has made a contribution to the trust by way of transfer, assignment or other disposition of property;

Definition.Text: TFSA, being a tax-free savings account, has the meaning assigned by subsection 146.2(5);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (d) of the definition Canadian real, immovable or resource property in subsection 248(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a corporation, an income or capital interest in a trust or an interest in a partnership (other than a taxable Canadian corporation, a SIFT trust or a SIFT partnership), if more than 50% of the fair market value of the share or interest is derived directly or indirectly from one or any combination of properties described in paragraphs (a) to (c), or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 248(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: foreign currency debt has the meaning assigned by subsection 111(8);

MarginalNote.DefinedTermFr: fait lié à la conversion d’une EIPD-fiducie

Definition.Text: SIFT trust wind-up event means a distribution by a particular trust resident in Canada of property to a taxpayer in respect of which the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the distribution occurs before 2013,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there is a resulting disposition of all of the taxpayer’s interest as a beneficiary under the particular trust,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the particular trust is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a SIFT wind-up entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust whose only beneficiary throughout the period (referred to in this definition as the “qualifying period”) that begins on July 14, 2008 and that ends at the time of the distribution is another trust that throughout the qualifying period

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is resident in Canada, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is a SIFT wind-up entity or a trust described by this subparagraph, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust whose only beneficiary at the time of distribution is another trust that throughout the qualifying period

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is resident in Canada,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: is a SIFT wind-up entity or a trust described by subparagraph (ii), and

Clause.Label: (C)
