Subsection.Text: In determining the amount of a SIFT trust’s or SIFT partnership’s taxable SIFT distributions for a taxation year earned in a province

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: except as provided in paragraph (b), this Part applies in respect of the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership as though

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: each reference to “corporation” (other than in the expression “subsidiary controlled corporation”) were read as a reference to “SIFT trust” or “SIFT partnership”, as the case may be,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: each reference to “taxable income” were read as a reference to “taxable SIFT distributions”,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: each reference to “its incorporating documents or bylaws” were read as a reference to “the agreement governing the SIFT trust” or “the agreement governing the SIFT partnership”, as the case may be, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: “subsidiary controlled corporation” in respect of a SIFT trust or a SIFT partnership meant a corporation more than 50% of the issued share capital of which (having full voting rights under all circumstances) belongs to the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership, as the case may be; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 400(1), section 401, subsections 402(1) and (2) and sections 403 to 413 do not apply.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (4), in applying the definition provincial SIFT tax rate in subsection 248(1) of the Act in respect of a SIFT trust or SIFT partnership for a taxation year, the prescribed amount determined in respect of the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership for the taxation year is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership has no permanent establishment in a province in the taxation year, 0.10;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership has a permanent establishment in a province in the taxation year and has no permanent establishment outside that province in the taxation year, the decimal fraction equivalent of the general corporate income tax rate in the province for the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership has a permanent establishment in the taxation year in a province, and has a permanent establishment outside that province in the taxation year, the amount, expressed as a decimal fraction, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts, if any, each of which is in respect of a province in which the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership has a permanent establishment in the taxation year and is determined by the formula
is its taxable SIFT distributions for the taxation year earned in the province,
is its total taxable SIFT distributions for the taxation year, and
is the decimal fraction equivalent of the general corporate income tax rate in the province for the taxation year, and
is the amount determined by the formula

is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined under the description of C in the description of A in respect of a province in which the SIFT trust or SIFT partnership has a permanent establishment in the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: If a SIFT trust or a SIFT partnership has a permanent establishment in Quebec in a taxation year, paragraph (a) of the definition general corporate income tax rate in subsection (1) does not apply in determining the prescribed amount under subsection (3) in respect of the SIFT trust or the SIFT partnership for the taxation year for the purposes of applying the definition provincial SIFT tax rate in determining:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of the SIFT partnership, the amount of a dividend deemed by paragraph 96(1.11)(b) of the Act to have been received by it in the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of the SIFT trust, the amount of its taxable SIFT trust distributions for the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2007 and subsequent taxation years, except that paragraph 414(4)(b), as enacted by subsection (1), shall not apply for the taxation years of a SIFT trust that end before February 3, 2009.
The heading “Policy Reserves for Pre-1996 Policies” before section 1401 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
The portion of subsection 1401(1) of the Regulations before subparagraph (c)(i) is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of section 307 of the Regulations and subsection 211.1(3) of the Act, the amounts determined under this subsection are,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of deposit administration fund policies, the aggregate of the insurer’s liabilities under those policies calculated in the manner required for the purposes of the insurer’s annual report to the relevant authority for the year or, where the insurer was throughout the year subject to the supervision of the relevant authority but was not required to file an annual report with the relevant authority for the year, in its financial statements for the year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a group term life insurance policy that provides coverage for a period not exceeding 12 months, the unearned portion of the premium paid by the policyholder for the policy at the end of the year determined by apportioning the premium paid by the policyholder equally over the period to which that premium pertains;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a life insurance policy, other than a policy referred to in paragraph (a) or (b), the greater of

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 1401(1)(c.1) of the Regulations before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a group life insurance policy, the amount (other than an amount in respect of which a deduction may be claimed by the insurer pursuant to subsection 140(1) of the Act because of subparagraph 138(3)(a)(v) of the Act in computing its income for the year) in respect of a dividend, refund of premiums or refund of premium deposits provided for under the terms of the policy that will be used by the insurer to reduce or eliminate a future adverse claims experience under the policy or that will be paid or unconditionally credited to the policyholder by the insurer or applied in discharge, in whole or in part, of a liability of the policyholder to pay premiums to the insurer, which is the least of

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 1401(1)(d) of the Regulations before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in respect of a policy, other than a policy referred to in paragraph (a), in respect of a benefit, risk or guarantee that is

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 1401(1)(d) of the Regulations after subparagraph (ix) and before subparagraph (x) is replaced by the following:
Subsection 1401(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (c.1) and by repealing paragraphs (d.1) to (e).
Subsection 1401(1.1) of the Regulations is repealed.
Subsections 1401(3) and (4) of the Regulations are repealed.
Subsections (1) to (7) apply to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
The headings before section 1404 and sections 1404 and 1405 of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

For the purpose of subparagraph 138(3)(a)(i) of the Act, there may be deducted, in computing a life insurer’s income from carrying on its life insurance business in Canada for a taxation year in respect of its life insurance policies in Canada, the amount the insurer claims, not exceeding

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined under subsection (3) in respect of the insurer for the year, where that amount is greater than nil; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: nil, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of paragraph 138(4)(b) of the Act, the amount prescribed in respect of an insurer for a taxation year, in respect of its life insurance policies in Canada, is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the absolute value of the amount determined under subsection (3) in respect of the insurer for the year, where that amount is less than nil; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: nil, in any other case.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraphs (1)(a) and (2)(a), the amount determined under this subsection in respect of an insurer for a taxation year, in respect of its life insurance policies in Canada, is the amount, which may be positive or negative, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount (except to the extent the amount is determined in respect of a claim, premium, dividend or refund in respect of which an amount is included in determining the value of B, C or D), in respect of the insurer’s life insurance policies in Canada, equal to the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of the reported reserves of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of those policies, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of the policy liabilities of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of those policies;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount, in respect of the insurer’s life insurance policies in Canada under which there may be claims incurred before the end of the year that have not been reported to the insurer before the end of the year, equal to 95% of the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of the reported reserves of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of the possibility that there are such claims, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of the policy liabilities of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of the possibility that there are such claims;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the unearned portion at the end of the year of the premium paid by the policyholder for the policy, determined by apportioning the premium paid by the policyholder equally over the period to which that premium relates, where the policy is a group term life insurance policy that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: provides coverage for a period that does not exceed 12 months, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: is a life insurance policy in Canada;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts (other than an amount deductible under subparagraph 138(3)(a)(v) of the Act) each of which is the amount, which is the least of P, Q and R, in respect of a dividend, refund of premiums or refund of premium deposits provided for under the terms of a group life insurance policy that is a life insurance policy in Canada that will be

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: used by the insurer to reduce or eliminate a future adverse claims experience under the policy,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: paid or unconditionally credited to the policyholder by the insurer, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: applied in discharge, in whole or in part, of a liability of the policyholder to pay premiums to the insurer under the policy,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is a reasonable amount as a reserve determined as at the end of the year in respect of the dividend, refund of premiums or refund of premium deposits provided for under the terms of the policy,
is 25% of the amount of the premium under the terms of the policy for the 12-month period ending

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: on the day the policy is terminated, if the policy is terminated in the year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: at the end of the year, in any other case, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the reported reserve of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of the dividend, refund of premiums or refund of premium deposits provided for under the terms of the policy; and
is the total of all amounts determined in respect of a life insurance policy in Canada each of which is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an amount payable in respect of a policy loan under the policy, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: interest that has accrued to the insurer to the end of the year in respect of a policy loan under the policy.

Section.Text: For the purpose of subparagraph 138(3)(a)(ii) of the Act, there may be deducted, in computing a life insurer’s income for a taxation year, the amount it claims as a reserve in respect of an unpaid claim received by the insurer before the end of the year under a life insurance policy in Canada, not exceeding the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the reported reserve of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of the claim, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the policy liability of the insurer at the end of the year in respect of the claim.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
The definition life insurance policy in Canada in subsection 1408(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: life insurance policy in Canada has the same meaning as defined in subsection 138(12) of the Act. (police d’assurance-vie au Canada)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1408(1) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: life insurance policy has the same meaning as defined in subsection 138(12) of the Act. (police d’assurance-vie)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that begin after November 7, 2007.
Section 2400 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (7):

Provision.Text: A computation that is required to be made under this Part in respect of an insurer’s taxation year that included September 30, 2006 and that is relevant to a computation (in this subsection referred to as the “transition year computation”) that is required to be made under this Part in respect of the insurer’s first taxation year that begins after that date shall, for the purposes only of the transition year computation, be made using the same definitions, rules and methodologies that are used in the transition year computation.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after September 2006.
Subsection 2600(1) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
In applying the definition income earned in the year in a province in subsection 120(4) of the Act for an individual’s taxation year

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the prescribed rules referred to in that definition are the rules in this Part; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined under those prescribed rules means the total of all amounts each of which is the individual’s income earned in the taxation year in a particular province as determined under this Part.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 3504:
The following conditions are prescribed in respect of a donee for the purposes of paragraph 110.1(8)(e) of the Act:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the donee has applied to the Minister for International Cooperation (or, if there is no such Minister, the Minister responsible for the Canadian International Development Agency) for a determination that the conditions described in this section have been met;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: medicines received by the donee for use in charitable activities outside Canada are

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: delivered outside Canada by the donee for use in its charitable activities, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: transferred to another registered charity that would meet the conditions contained in this section if that registered charity were a donee described in subsection 110.1(8) of the Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the course of delivering medicines outside Canada for use in its charitable activities, the donee acts in a manner consistent with the principles and objectives of the inter-agency Guidelines for Drug Donations issued by the World Health Organization, as amended from time to time, (referred to in this section as “the WHO Guidelines”);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the donee has sufficient expertise in delivering medicines for use in charitable activities carried on outside Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the donee carries on a program that includes delivering medicines for use in charitable activities carried on outside Canada and that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an international development assistance program, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an international humanitarian assistance program, responding to situations of international humanitarian crisis (resulting from either natural disaster or complex emergency); and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the donee has sufficient expertise to design, implement and monitor each program described in subparagraph (e)(i) or (ii) that it carries on, unless the donee has declared that it will not deliver medicines in that program.

Subsection.Text: Without limiting the application of the WHO Guidelines, for the purposes of paragraph (1)(c), a donee does not act in a manner consistent with the principles and objectives of those guidelines if the donee’s directors, trustees, officers or like officials have not

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: approved a policy and procedural framework, under which the donee is required to act in a manner consistent with the WHO Guidelines; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: declared that the donee acts in compliance with that policy and procedural framework.

Subsection.Text: A donee is considered not to have sufficient expertise for the purpose of a program to which paragraph (1)(d) or (e) applies if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the program does not address the specific and differentiated needs, interests and vulnerabilities of affected women and men, girls and boys;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the program does not incorporate, in the design of projects under the program, consideration for environmental effects of those projects; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the donee does not have policies and practices for the design, implementation and monitoring of the program.

Subsection.Text: The Minister referred to in subsection (1) may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: rely on any information or evidence in making a determination under subsection (1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: require the donee to provide any other information or evidence that that Minister considers relevant and sufficient for the purpose of this section.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of applications made at any time by donees for determinations in respect of gifts made after June 2008.
The portion of subsection 4900(1) of the Regulations before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph (d) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 146(1) of the Act, paragraph (e) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 146.1(1) of the Act, paragraph (c) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 146.3(1) of the Act, paragraph (h) of the definition qualified investment in section 204 of the Act, paragraph (d) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 205(1) of the Act and paragraph (c) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, each of the following investments is prescribed as a qualified investment for a plan trust at a particular time if at that time it is
Paragraph 4900(1)(i.3) of the Regulations is repealed.
Subparagraph 4900(1)(j.2)(ii) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the certificate has, at the time of acquisition by the plan trust, an investment grade rating with a credit rating agency referred to in subsection (2), and

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 4900(1)(k) and (l) of the Regulations are repealed.
Paragraph 4900(1)(o) of the Regulations is repealed.
Paragraph 4900(1)(s) of the Regulations is repealed.
Subsection 4900(1) of the Regulations is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (u), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (v) and by adding the following after paragraph (v):

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: an American Depositary Receipt where the property represented by the receipt is listed on a designated stock exchange.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4900(5) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of paragraph (e) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 146.1(1) of the Act, paragraph (d) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 205(1) of the Act and paragraph (c) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, a property is prescribed as a qualified investment for a trust governed by a registered disability savings plan, a registered education savings plan or a TFSA at any time if at that time the property is an interest in a trust or a share of the capital stock of a corporation that was a registered investment for a trust governed by a registered retirement savings plan during the calendar year in which that time occurs or during the preceding year.
Paragraph 4900(12)(a) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property was a share of the capital stock of a specified small business corporation,

Subsection.Text: Section 4900 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (13):

For the purposes of paragraph (c) of the definition qualified investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, a property is prescribed as a qualified investment for a trust governed by a TFSA at any time if, at the time the property was acquired by the trust, the property

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a specified small business corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a venture capital corporation described in any of sections 6700 to 6700.2, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a qualifying share in respect of a specified cooperative corporation and the TFSA; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: was not a prohibited investment for the trust.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (8) to (10) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsections (2) to (6) apply to property acquired after the day on which this Act is assented to.

Subsection (7) applies in determining whether a property is, at any time after 2005, a qualified investment, except that in applying paragraph 4900(1)(w) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (7), before December 14, 2007, it is to be read as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (w)
Paragraph.Text: an American Depositary Receipt where the property represented by the receipt is listed on a stock exchange referred to in section 3200 or 3201.

Subsection.Text: The definitions governing plan and qualifying share in subsection 4901(2) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: governing plan means a deferred profit sharing plan or a revoked plan, a registered disability savings plan, a registered education savings plan, a registered retirement income fund, a registered retirement savings plan or a TFSA; (régime d’encadrement)
qualifying share, in respect of a specified cooperative corporation and a governing plan, means a share of the capital or capital stock of the corporation where

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: ownership of the share or a share identical to the share is not a condition of membership in the corporation, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a connected person under the governing plan

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: has not received a payment from the corporation pursuant to an allocation in proportion to patronage in respect of consumer goods or services, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: can reasonably be expected not to receive a payment, after the acquisition of the share by the plan trust, from the corporation pursuant to an allocation in proportion to patronage in respect of consumer goods or services; (part admissible)

Subsection.Text: The definition connected person in subsection 4901(2) of the English version of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: connected person under a governing plan of a plan trust means a person who is an annuitant, a beneficiary, an employer or a subscriber under, or a holder of, the governing plan and any person who does not deal at arm’s length with that person; (personne rattachée)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 4901(2) of the Regulations is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: specified small business corporation, at any time, means a corporation (other than a cooperative corporation) that would, at that time or at the end of the last taxation year of the corporation that ended before that time, be a small business corporation if the expression “Canadian-controlled private corporation” in the definition small business corporation in subsection 248(1) of the Act were read as “Canadian corporation (other than a corporation controlled at that time, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, by one or more non-resident persons)”. (société déterminée exploitant une petite entreprise)

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (3) apply to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after Part XLIX:

Section.Text: For the purpose of the portion of the definition prohibited investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act before paragraph (a), property described in paragraph 4900(1)(j.1) is prescribed property.

For the purpose of paragraph (d) of the definition prohibited investment in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act, property that is a qualified investment for a trust governed by a TFSA solely because of subsection 4900(14) is prescribed property for the trust at any time if, at that time, it is not described in any of subparagraphs 4900(14)(a)(i) to (iii).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
The headings before section 5700 of Regulations are replaced by the following:
Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on February 27, 2008.
Section 5700 of the Regulations is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (y) and by adding the following after paragraph (z):

Paragraph.Label: (z.1)
Paragraph.Text: altered auditory feedback device designed to be used by an individual who has a speech impairment;

Paragraph.Label: (z.2)
Paragraph.Text: electrotherapy device designed to be used by an individual with a medical condition or by an individual who has a severe mobility impairment;

Paragraph.Label: (z.3)
Paragraph.Text: standing device designed to be used by an individual who has a severe mobility impairment to undertake standing therapy; and

Paragraph.Label: (z.4)
Paragraph.Text: pressure pulse therapy device designed to be used by an individual who has a balance disorder.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2008 and subsequent taxation years.
The Regulations are amended by adding the following after section 5700:

Section.Text: For the purpose of subparagraph 118.2(2)(n)(ii) of the Act, a drug, medicament or other preparation or substance is prescribed if it

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is manufactured, sold or represented for use in the diagnosis, treatment or prevention of a disease, disorder or abnormal physical state, or its symptoms, or in restoring, correcting or modifying an organic function;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is prescribed for a patient by a medical practitioner; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may, in the jurisdiction in which it is acquired, be lawfully acquired for use by the patient only with the intervention of a medical practitioner.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on February 27, 2008.
Subsection 5906(2) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:
For the purposes of subsection (1), the expression “permanent establishment” has:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the expression is given a particular meaning in a tax treaty with a country, the meaning assigned by that tax treaty with respect to a business carried on in that country; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the meaning that would be assigned by subsection 400(2) if that subsection were read without reference to its paragraph (e.1).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2009 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 5907(11) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, a sovereign state or other jurisdiction is a designated treaty country for a taxation year of a foreign affiliate of a corporation if Canada has entered into a comprehensive agreement or convention for the elimination of double taxation on income, or a comprehensive tax information exchange agreement, in respect of that sovereign state or jurisdiction, that has entered into force and has effect for that taxation year, but any territory, possession, department, dependency or area of that sovereign state or jurisdiction to which that agreement or convention does not apply is not considered to be part of that sovereign state or jurisdiction for the purpose of determining whether it is a designated treaty country.
Section 5907 of the Regulations is further amended by adding the following after subsection (11.1):

For the purpose of applying subsection (11) in respect of a foreign affiliate of a corporation, where a comprehensive tax information exchange agreement enters into force on a particular day, the agreement is deemed to enter into force and to come into effect on the first day of the foreign affiliate’s taxation year that includes the particular day.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply after 2007.
Paragraphs 7305.1(a) and (b) of the Regulations are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxpayer is employed in a taxation year by a particular person principally in selling or leasing automobiles and an automobile is made available in the year to the taxpayer or a person related to the taxpayer by the particular person or a person related to the particular person, 21 cents; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, 24 cents.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after 2007.
Paragraph 7306(a) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the product of 46 cents multiplied by the number of those kilometres;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to kilometres driven after 2007.
Section 8308.1 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

For the purposes of applying subsection (2) in determining an individual’s pension credit for a calendar year with respect to an employer under a foreign plan, if any contributions made to, or benefits accruing under, the plan in respect of the individual and the calendar year benefit from the application of paragraph 8 of Article XVIII of the Canada-United States Tax Convention signed at Washington on September 26, 1980, or from the application of a similar provision in another tax treaty,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subparagraph (2)(b)(ii) shall be read without reference to the words “was resident in Canada and”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection (2) after subparagraph (b)(iv) shall be read as “the lesser of the money purchase limit for the year and 18% of the individual’s resident compensation from the employer for the year”.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in determining pension credits for the 2009 and subsequent calendar years.
Section 8308.2 of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the descriptions of B in the definitions RRSP deduction limit and unused RRSP deduction room in subsection 146(1) of the Act and the description of B in paragraph 204.2(1.1)(b) of the Act, there is prescribed in respect of an individual for a calendar year the lesser of the money purchase limit for the preceding calendar year (in this section referred to as the “service year”) and the amount determined by subsection (2), if the individual

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: rendered services to an employer (excluding services that were primarily services rendered in Canada or services rendered in connection with a business carried on by the employer in Canada, or a combination of those services) throughout a period in the service year in which the individual was resident in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: became entitled, either absolutely or contingently, in the service year to benefits under a foreign plan (as defined in subsection 8308.1(1)) in respect of the services; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: continued to be entitled at the end of the service year, either absolutely or contingently, to all or part of the benefits.

Subsection.Text: The amount determined for the purpose of subsection (1) is,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the only benefits to which the individual became entitled in the service year under the foreign plan were provided under one or more money purchase provisions of the foreign plan, the total of all amounts each of which is the individual’s pension credit for the service year with respect to the employer under a money purchase provision of the foreign plan, determined

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: as though the foreign plan were a registered pension plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: without regard to any contributions made by the individual, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if, under the laws of the country in which the foreign plan is established, any contributions made after the end of the service year are treated as having been made in the service year, as though those contributions were made in the service year and not when the contributions were actually made; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the total that would be determined under paragraph (a) if the individual had not become entitled in the service year to any benefits under a defined benefit provision of the foreign plan, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 10% of the portion of the individual’s resident compensation from the employer for the service year that is attributable to services rendered to the employer and included under paragraph (1)(a).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in determining prescribed amounts for the 2009 and subsequent calendar years except that in determining prescribed amounts for 2009, the amount of the money purchase limit for 2008 is deemed to be reduced by $600.
Subsection 8506(7) of the Regulations and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: The minimum amount for a member’s account under a money purchase provision of a registered pension plan for a calendar year is

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: nil, if an individual who is either the member or the specified beneficiary of the member for the year in relation to the provision

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: is alive at the beginning of the year, and

Provision.Label: (ii)
Provision.Text: had not attained 71 years of age at the end of the preceding calendar year; and

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: if paragraph (a) does not apply and the year is 2008, 75 per cent of the amount that would, in the absence of this subsection, be the minimum amount for the account for the year.

Subsection.Text: Section 8506 of the Regulations is amended by adding the following after subsection (8):

Provision.Text: If a contribution made by a member of a registered pension plan and credited to the member’s account under a money purchase provision of the plan complies with the conditions in subsection (10), the contribution

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: is deemed to have been made in accordance with the plan as registered;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: is to be disregarded for the purposes of paragraph (2)(c.1); and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: is deemed to be an excluded contribution for the purposes of paragraph 8301(4)(a).
The conditions referred to in subsection (9) are as follows:

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: the contribution is made in 2008;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: the contribution is designated for the purposes of this subsection in a manner acceptable to the Minister; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: the amount of the contribution does not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of a retirement benefit (other than a retirement benefit permissible under any of paragraphs (1)(a) to (e)) paid from the plan in 2008 in respect of the account and included, because of paragraph 56(1)(a) of the Act, in computing the taxpayer’s income for the taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount that would, in the absence of paragraph (7)(b), be the minimum amount for the account for 2008,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the minimum amount for the account for 2008, and
is the total of all other contributions made by the member under the money purchase provision at or before the time of the contribution and designated for the purposes of this subsection.

Subsection.Text: Contributions described in subsections 8506(9) and (10) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), that are made during the period that begins after 2008 and ends on the day that is 30 days after the day on which this Act is assented to (or such longer period as is acceptable to the Minister of National Revenue) are deemed for the purpose of subsection 8506(10) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), to have been made on December 31, 2008 and not when they were actually made, except that the amounts so deemed shall not exceed the amount that would be determined in respect of the account under paragraph 8506(10)(c) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), if the value of C in the formula in that paragraph were nil.
Part XC of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

Heading.Label: PART XC
Heading.TitleText: FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS — PRESCRIBED ENTITIES AND PROPERTIES
Prescribed Trust not a Financial Institution

Section.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (e) of the definition financial institution in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a trust is, at any particular time, a prescribed person if the following conditions are satisfied at that particular time:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the trust is a related segregated fund trust (within the meaning assigned by paragraph 138.1(1)(a) of the Act);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trust is deemed, under paragraph 138.1(1)(a) of the Act, to have been created at a time that is not more than two years before that particular time; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the cost of the trustee’s interest (as determined by paragraph 138.1(1)(c) and (d) of the Act) in the trust does not exceed $5,000,000.

Subsection.Text: In this section, qualified small business corporation, at any time, means a corporation in respect of which the following conditions are satisfied at that time:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation is a Canadian-controlled private corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation either is an eligible corporation (as defined in subsection 5100(1)) or would be an eligible corporation if the definition eligible corporation in subsection 5100(1) were read without reference to its paragraph (e);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the carrying value of the total assets of the corporation and all corporations related to it (determined in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles on a consolidated or combined basis, where applicable) does not exceed $50,000,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the number of employees of the corporation and all corporations related to it does not exceed 500.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (e) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a share of the capital stock of a corporation is a prescribed property of a taxpayer if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: immediately after the time at which the taxpayer acquired the share, the corporation was a qualified small business corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation continued to be a qualified small business corporation for one year after that time, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer could not reasonably expect at that time that the corporation would cease to be a qualified small business corporation within one year after that time; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the share was issued to the taxpayer in exchange for one or more shares of the capital stock of the corporation that were, at the time of the exchange, prescribed property of the taxpayer under this subsection.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (e) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, and of subparagraph 142.6(4)(a)(ii) of the Act, a debt obligation held by a bank is a prescribed property of the bank if the obligation is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an exposure to a designated country (within the meaning assigned by section 8006);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a United Mexican States Collateralized Par Bond due 2019; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a United Mexican States Collateralized Discount Bond due 2019.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (e) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a share is a prescribed property of a taxpayer for a taxation year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the share is a lending asset of the taxpayer in the year; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the share was, immediately after its issuance, a share described in paragraph (e) of the definition term preferred share in subsection 248(1) of the Act, and the share would, at any time in the year, be a term preferred share if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: that definition were read without reference to the portion following paragraph (b), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: where the share was issued or acquired on or before June 28, 1982, it were issued or acquired after that day.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (e) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a share of the capital stock of a corporation that is held by a credit union is a prescribed property of the credit union for a taxation year if, throughout that taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation is a credit union; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: credit unions hold

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of the corporation that give the credit unions more than 50% of the votes that could be cast under all circumstances at an annual meeting of shareholders of the corporation, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: shares of the corporation having a fair market value of more than 50% of the fair market value of all the issued shares of the corporation.

Heading.TitleText: Prescribed Payment Card Corporation Share not Mark-to-Market Property

Section.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (b) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a prescribed payment card corporation share of a taxpayer at any time means a share of the capital stock of a particular corporation if, at that time,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the particular corporation is any one of the following

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: MasterCard International Incorporated,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: MasterCard Incorporated, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: Visa Inc.; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the share

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is of a class of shares that is not listed on a stock exchange,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is not convertible into or exchangeable for a share of the class of the capital stock of a corporation that is listed on a stock exchange, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: was issued by the particular corporation to the taxpayer or to a person related to the taxpayer.

Heading.TitleText: Prescribed Securities Exchange Investment not Mark-to-Market Property

Section.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (c) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a prescribed securities exchange investment of a taxpayer at any time means a share of the capital stock of a corporation if, at that time, the corporation is not a public corporation and is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: The Toronto Stock Exchange Inc.;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: TSX Inc.;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: TSX Group Inc.;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: Bourse de Montréal Inc.; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: Canadian Venture Exchange Inc.

Section.Text: For the purpose of paragraph 142.2(3)(c) of the Act, a share described in paragraph 9002(2)(b) is prescribed in respect of all taxpayers.

Heading.TitleText: Financing Arrangement not a Specified Debt Obligation

Section.Text: For the purpose of paragraph (c) of the definition specified debt obligation in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a property is a prescribed property throughout a taxation year if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the property is a direct financing lease, or any other financing arrangement, of a taxpayer that is reported as a loan in the taxpayer’s financial statements for the year prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year, an amount is deductible under paragraph 20(1)(a) of the Act in respect of the property that is the subject of the arrangement.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 9002(3) of the Regulations is replaced by the following:

For the purpose of paragraph (e) of the definition excluded property in subsection 142.2(1) of the Act, a share of the capital stock of a corporation that is held by a credit union is a prescribed property of the credit union for a taxation year if, throughout the period (referred to in this subsection as the “holding period”) in that taxation year during which the credit union holds the share

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation is a credit union; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the following conditions are satisfied:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: credit unions hold shares of the corporation that

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: give those credit unions at least 50% of the votes that could be cast under all circumstances at an annual meeting of shareholders of the corporation, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: have a fair market value of at least 50% of the fair market value of all the issued shares of the corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation is not controlled, directly or indirectly in any manner whatever, by any person that is not a credit union, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the corporation would not be controlled by a person that is not a credit union if each share of the corporation that is not owned at any time in the holding period by a credit union were owned, at that time, by the person.

Subsection.Text: Section 9002.2 of the Regulations is repealed.
The portion of Part XC before section 9002.2 and section 9003 of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), apply to taxation years that end after February 22, 1994, except that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in applying sections 9001 and 9002 of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), to taxation years that begin before October 2006, the references in those sections to “excluded property” are to be read as references to “mark-to-market property”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in applying section 9002.1 of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), to taxation years that begin before October 2006, the reference in that section to “paragraph (b) of the definition excluded property” is to be read as a reference to “paragraph (d.1) of the definition mark-to-market property”.

Subsection.Text: Section 9002.2 of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to taxation years that begin after 1998 and before 2008, except that in applying that section to taxation years that begin before October 2006, the reference in that section to “paragraph (c) of the definition excluded property” is to be read as a reference to “paragraph (d.2) of the definition mark-to-market property”.
Section 9004 of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (1), applies to taxation years that begin after February 2, 2009.
Subsection 9002(3) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), applies to taxation years that begin after February 2, 2009, except that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it also applies to taxation years, of a taxpayer, that end after 2002 and begin before February 3, 2009, if the taxpayer so elects in writing and files the election with the Minister of National Revenue on or before the taxpayer’s filing-due date for the taxpayer’s taxation year in which this Act is assented to; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a taxpayer makes an election under paragraph (a), in applying subsection 9002(3) of the Regulations, as enacted by subsection (2), for taxation years of the taxpayer that begin before October 2006, the reference in that subsection to “excluded property” is to be read as a reference to “mark-to-market property”.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (3) applies to taxation years that begin after 2007.

Heading.Label: PART 2
Heading.TitleText: AMENDMENTS IN RESPECT OF SALES AND EXCISE TAXES

Section.Text: The portion of the definition officer in section 2 of the English version of the Excise Act, 2001 before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: officer means, except in section 167, in the definition contact information in subsection 211(1) and in sections 226 and 296,

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition confidential information in subsection 211(1) of the Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: It excludes information that does not directly or indirectly reveal the identity of the person to whom it relates and, for the purposes of applying subsections (3), (8) and (9) to a representative of a government entity that is not an official, it includes only the information referred to in paragraph (6)(j).

Subsection.Text: Subsection 211(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: aboriginal government means an aboriginal government as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act.

contact information, in respect of a holder of a business number, means the name, address, telephone number, facsimile number and preferred language of communication of the holder, or similar information as specified by the Minister in respect of the holder, and includes such information in respect of one or more

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: trustees of the holder, if the holder is a trust;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: members of the holder, if the holder is a partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: officers of the holder, if the holder is a corporation; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: officers or members of the holder, in any other case.

Definition.Text: corporate information, in respect of a holder of a business number that is a corporation, means the name (including the number assigned by the incorporating authority), date of incorporation, jurisdiction of incorporation and any information on the dissolution, reorganization, amalgamation, winding-up or revival of the corporation.
government entity means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a department or agency of the government of Canada or of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a municipality;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an aboriginal government;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation all of the shares (except directors’ qualifying shares) of the capital stock of which are owned by one or more persons each of which is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of a province,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a municipality, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation described in this paragraph; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a board or commission, established by Her Majesty or Her Majesty in right of a province, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of government, or by a municipality, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of a municipality.

Definition.Text: municipality means an incorporated city, town, village, metropolitan authority, township, district, county or rural municipality or other incorporated municipal body however designated.
registration information, in respect of a holder of a business number, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any information pertaining to the legal form of the holder;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the type of activities carried on or proposed to be carried on by the holder;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each date on which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the business number was issued to the holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder began activities,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder ceased or resumed activities, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the business number assigned to the holder was changed; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons for the cessation, resumption or change referred to in subparagraph (c)(iii) or (iv).

Definition.Text: representative of a government entity means a person who is employed in the service of, who occupies a position of responsibility in the service of, or who is engaged by or on behalf of, a government entity, and includes, for the purposes of subsections (2), (3), (8) and (9), a person who was formerly so employed, who formerly occupied such a position or who formerly was so engaged.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 211(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Except as authorized under this section, no official or other representative of a government entity shall knowingly
Subsection 211(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite any other Act of Parliament or other law, no official or other representative of a government entity shall be required, in connection with any legal proceedings, to give or produce evidence relating to any confidential information.
Paragraph 211(6)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (6.1), provide to a representative of a government entity the business number of, the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), and any contact information, corporate information and registration information in respect of, the holder of a business number, if the information is provided solely for the purpose of the administration or enforcement of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an Act of Parliament or of a legislature of a province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a by-law of a municipality or a law of an aboriginal government;

Subsection.Text: Section 211 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

No information may be provided to a representative of a government entity under paragraph (6)(j) in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the government entity unless the government entity uses the business number as an identifier in connection with the program, activity or service.

The Minister may, in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the Minister, make available to the public the business number of, and the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), the holder of a business number.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Public disclosure by representative of government entity
Subsection.Text: A representative of a government entity may, in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the government entity, make available to the public the business number of, and the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), the holder of a business number, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a representative of the government entity was provided with that information pursuant to paragraph (6)(j); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the government entity uses the business number as an identifier in connection with the program, activity or service.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 211(8) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure to person or on consent
Subsection.Text: An official or other representative of a government entity may provide confidential information relating to a person
The portion of subsection 211(9) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An order or direction that is made in the course of or in connection with any legal proceedings and that requires an official or other representative of a government entity to give or produce evidence relating to any confidential information may, by notice served on all interested parties, be appealed immediately by the Minister or by the person against whom it is made to
The portion of the definition confidential information in subsection 295(1) of the Excise Tax Act after paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

ContinuedDefinition.Text: but does not include information that does not directly or indirectly reveal the identity of the person to whom it relates and, for the purposes of applying subsections (3), (6) and (7) to a representative of a government entity who is not an official, includes only the information described in paragraph (5)(j);

Subsection.Text: Subsection 295(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: aboriginal government means an aboriginal government as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act;

contact information, in respect of a holder of a business number, means the name, address, telephone number, facsimile number and preferred language of communication of the holder, or similar information as specified by the Minister in respect of the holder, and includes such information in respect of one or more

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: trustees of the holder, if the holder is a trust,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: members of the holder, if the holder is a partnership,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: officers of the holder, if the holder is a corporation, or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: officers or members of the holder, in any other case;

Definition.Text: corporate information, in respect of a holder of a business number that is a corporation, means the name (including the number assigned by the incorporating authority), date of incorporation, jurisdiction of incorporation and any information on the dissolution, reorganization, amalgamation, winding-up or revival of the corporation;
government entity means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a department or agency of the government of Canada or of a province,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a municipality,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an aboriginal government,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation all of the shares (except directors’ qualifying shares) of the capital stock of which are owned by one or more persons each of which is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of Canada,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: Her Majesty in right of a province,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a municipality, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation described in this paragraph, or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a board or commission, established by Her Majesty in right of Canada or Her Majesty in right of a province, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of government, or by a municipality, that performs an administrative or regulatory function of a municipality;

Definition.Text: municipality does not include a local authority determined by the Minister to be a municipality under paragraph (b) of the definition municipality in subsection 123(1);
registration information, in respect of a holder of a business number, means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any information pertaining to the legal form of the holder,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the type of activities carried on or proposed to be carried on by the holder,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: each date on which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the business number was issued to the holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder began activities,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder ceased or resumed activities, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the business number assigned to the holder was changed, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons for the cessation, resumption or change referred to in subparagraph (c)(iii) or (iv);

Definition.Text: representative of a government entity means a person who is employed in the service of, who occupies a position of responsibility in the service of, or who is engaged by or on behalf of, a government entity, and includes, for the purposes of subsections (2), (3), (6) and (7), a person who was formerly so employed, who formerly occupied such a position or who formerly was so engaged.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 295(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Except as authorized under this section, no official or other representative of a government entity shall knowingly
Subsection 295(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite any other Act of Parliament or other law, no official or other representative of a government entity shall be required, in connection with any legal proceedings, to give or produce evidence relating to any confidential information.
Subparagraph 295(5)(d)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to an official of the Department of Finance solely for the purposes of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the formulation or evaluation of fiscal policy,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: an administration agreement, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act, entered into with an aboriginal government, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: an administration agreement, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the First Nations Goods and Services Tax Act,

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 295(5)(j) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (5.01), provide to a representative of a government entity the business number of, the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), and any contact information, corporate information and registration information in respect of, the holder of a business number, if the information is provided solely for the purpose of the administration or enforcement of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an Act of Parliament or of a legislature of a province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a by-law of a municipality or a law of an aboriginal government;

Subsection.Text: Section 295 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

No information may be provided to a representative of a government entity under paragraph (5)(j) in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the government entity unless the government entity uses the business number as an identifier in connection with the program, activity or service.

The Minister may, in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the Minister, make available to the public the business number of, and the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), the holder of a business number.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Public disclosure by representative of government entity
Subsection.Text: A representative of a government entity may, in connection with a program, activity or service provided or undertaken by the government entity, make available to the public the business number of, and the name of (including any trade name or other name used by), the holder of a business number, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a representative of the government entity was provided with that information pursuant to paragraph (5)(j); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the government entity uses the business number as an identifier in connection with the program, activity or service.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 295(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure to person or on consent
Subsection.Text: An official or other representative of a government entity may provide confidential information relating to a person
The portion of subsection 295(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

An order or direction that is made in the course of or in connection with any legal proceedings and that requires an official or other representative of a government entity to give or produce evidence relating to any confidential information may, by notice served on all interested parties, be appealed forthwith by the Minister or by the person against whom the order or direction is made to

Section.Text: Paragraph 133(c) of the Customs Tariff is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of tariff item No. 9801.10.10, 9801.10.20, 9801.20.00, 9808.00.00 or 9810.00.00, prescribing conditions under which goods may be imported;

Section.Text: Chapter 4 of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by adding the following after Supplementary Note 2:

Provision.Text: Milk protein substances with a milk protein content of less than 85% by weight, calculated on the dry matter, are classified in tariff item No. 0404.90.10 or 0404.90.20. Milk protein substances with a milk protein content of 85% or more by weight, calculated on the dry matter, are classified in Chapter 35 (subheading 3504.00).

Section.Text: Tariff item No. 0404.90.10 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing in the column “Most-Favoured Nation Tariff / Final Rate” the reference to “6.5% (B)” with a reference to “3% (F)”.
Chapter 35 of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by adding the following after Note 2:

Provision.Text: Milk protein substances with a milk protein content of 85% or more by weight, calculated on the dry matter, are classified in tariff item No. 3504.00.11 or 3504.00.12. Milk protein substances with a milk protein content of less than 85% by weight, calculated on the dry matter, are classified in Chapter 4 (subheading 0404.90).

Section.Text: Tariff item No. 3504.00.00 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is repealed.
Chapter 35 of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in numerical order, the tariff provisions set out in Schedule 1 to this Act.
The following tariff provisions of the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act are repealed:

Section.Text: Tariff item No. 8406.90.32 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing in the column “Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff / Final Rate” the reference to “6.5% (A)” with a reference to “Free (F)”.

Tariff item No. 8406.90.34 in the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by replacing in the column “Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff / Final Rate” the reference to “6.5% (A)” with a reference to “Free (F)”.

Sections 123 to 127 and subsection 220(1) are deemed to have come into force on September 8, 2008.
Sections 122 and 128 to 219 and subsection 220(2) are deemed to have come into force on January 28, 2009.

Section.Text: Section 66 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsections (1) to (3), the premium rate for the year 2010 is 1.73%.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 73:

There shall be credited to the Employment Insurance Account on August 1, 2010 the amount determined by the Minister of Finance that corresponds to the cost of the benefit enhancement measures under this Act, provided for in the budget tabled in Parliament on January 27, 2009 in which the cost is estimated to be $2,900,000,000.

Subsection.Text: Schedule I to the Act is replaced by the Schedule I set out in Schedule 8 to this Act.
Schedule I to the Act is replaced by the Schedule I set out in Schedule 9 to this Act.

Section.Text: The maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid to a claimant referred to in subsection 55(7) of the Employment Insurance Regulations whose benefit period has not ended before the second Sunday before the day on which this Act receives royal assent and does not begin after September 11, 2010 is to be determined in accordance with Schedule 10.

Heading.TitleText: Pilot Project Relating To Extended Benefits

Subsection.Text: Section 77.6 of the Employment Insurance Regulations is deemed to cease to have effect on the second Saturday before the day on which this Act receives royal assent.

The maximum number of weeks for which benefits may be paid in a benefit period that is established for a claimant who is included in Pilot Project No. 10 and whose benefit period has not ended before the second Sunday before the day on which this Act receives royal assent is to be determined in accordance with Schedule I to the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by subsection 224(1).

Heading.TitleText: Premium Rates Provided for Under the Employment Insurance Act

Section.Text: Section 66.1 of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by section 9 of chapter 5 of the Statutes of Canada, 2001, is deemed to have read as follows:
Notwithstanding section 66, the premium rates for the years 2002 and 2003 are 2.2% and 2.1%, respectively.
Section 66.3 of the Employment Insurance Act, as enacted by section 25 of chapter 22 of the Statutes of Canada, 2004, is deemed to have read as follows:
Notwithstanding section 66, the premium rate for the year 2005 is 1.95%.

Subsection 224(1) applies with respect to every claimant whose benefit period has not ended before the day on which that subsection comes into force and whose benefit period does not begin after September 11, 2010.

Subsection.Text: In this section, “other Act” means the Budget Implementation Act, 2008.
If section 127 of the other Act comes into force before section 222 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that section 222 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 66 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), the premium rate for the year 2010 is 1.73%.

If section 222 of this Act comes into force before section 127 of the other Act, then, on the day on which that section 127 comes into force, section 66 of the Employment Insurance Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):
Despite subsection (1), the premium rate for the year 2010 is 1.73%.

If section 127 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 222 of this Act, then that section 222 is deemed to have come into force before that section 127 and subsection (3) applies as a consequence.
Subsection 224(1) is deemed to have come into force on the second Sunday before the day on which this Act receives royal assent.
Subsection 224(2) comes into force on September 12, 2010.

Heading.Label: PART 5
Heading.TitleText: STABILITY AND EFFICIENCY OF THE FINANCIAL SYSTEM

Section.Text: The Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following after section 60.1:

Heading.Label: PART IV.1
Heading.TitleText: STABILITY AND EFFICIENCY OF THE FINANCIAL SYSTEM

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: debt obligation means a bond, debenture, note or other evidence of indebtedness of an entity, whether secured or unsecured.
entity means an entity that, in the Minister’s opinion, is operating in Canada.
financial markets includes markets for money, bonds, equities, derivatives, foreign exchange and commodities.
financial system includes financial institutions, financial markets and payment systems as defined in section 36 of the Canadian Payments Act.
security means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to a corporation, a share, a class of shares or a debt obligation of the corporation, and includes any conversion or exchange privilege, option or other right to acquire a share of the corporation; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in relation to any other entity, any ownership interest in or debt obligation of the entity.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), the Minister may, with the Governor in Council’s authorization, enter into, on behalf of Her Majesty in right of Canada, any contract that in the Minister’s opinion is necessary to promote the stability or maintain the efficiency of the financial system in Canada, including such a contract to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: purchase, acquire, hold, lend or sell or otherwise dispose of securities of an entity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: create a charge on, or right or interest in, securities of an entity held by the Minister;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make a loan to an entity;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: provide a line of credit to an entity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: guarantee any debt, obligation or financial asset of an entity; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: provide loan insurance or credit insurance for the benefit of an entity in respect of any debt, obligation or financial asset of the entity.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (2)(a) does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shares, as defined in subsection 973.2(15) of the Bank Act, of a bank or bank holding company, as defined in section 2 of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shares, as defined in subsection 459.9(14) of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act, of an association as defined in section 2 of that Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shares, as defined in subsection 1016.7(15) of the Insurance Companies Act, of a company or insurance holding company, as defined in subsection 2(1) of that Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: shares, as defined in subsection 527.9(15) of the Trust and Loan Companies Act, of a company as defined in section 2 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 90 does not apply if the Minister purchases, acquires or sells or otherwise disposes, under paragraph (2)(a), of shares within the meaning of that section.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Section 61 and Surplus Crown Assets Act do not apply
Subsection.Text: Section 61 and the Surplus Crown Assets Act do not apply if the Minister holds, loans or sells or otherwise disposes of securities under paragraph (2)(a).

Any amount payable under or in connection with a contract entered into under this section may be paid out of the Consolidated Revenue Fund, on the requisition of the Minister, at the times and in the manner that the Minister considers appropriate.
This section applies to any contract entered into on or after November 30, 2008.

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: bridge institution means a federal institution that is designated as a bridge institution by an order made under paragraph 39.13(1)(c);

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, exempt the Corporation from the requirement that it pursue its objects in a manner that will minimize its exposure to loss when it takes any action to address a situation that is specified in the order.

The Governor in Council may make the order only if the Minister is of the opinion, after consultation with the Board, the Governor of the Bank of Canada and the Superintendent, that the requirement that the Corporation pursue its objects in a manner that will minimize its exposure to loss, in respect of a situation that will be specified in the order, might have an adverse effect on the stability of the financial system in Canada or public confidence in that stability.

The Governor in Council may repeal the order only if the Minister is of the opinion that the requirement that the Corporation pursue its objects in a manner that will minimize its exposure to loss, in respect of the situation specified in the order, will no longer have an adverse effect on the stability of the financial system in Canada or public confidence in that stability.
An order made under subsection 7.1(1) has effect from the time that it is made.
The Statutory Instruments Act does not apply to the order.

The Minister shall cause a notice to be published in the Canada Gazette that the order has been made or repealed as soon as the Minister is of the opinion that the publication of the notice will not have an adverse effect on the stability of the financial system in Canada or public confidence in that stability.

Section.Text: After the publication of a notice in the Canada Gazette that an order was made under subsection 7.1(1), the Corporation shall, in accordance with its by-laws, collect a special premium from member institutions or any class of member institutions in order to recover the loss that the Corporation determines it incurred as a result of pursuing its objects without regard to the requirement that it do so in a manner that minimizes its exposure to loss.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10:
